context: Rust Axum service with disciplined test infra. CI runs: cargo nextest with coverage at 85% on changed lines (hard-gated), cargo mutants weekly with ratchet-only gate, dependency-rules.json checked by a custom build script (hard-gated), test files included in clippy + rustfmt config, acceptance specs via a small bash harness against a docker-compose, all steps run via a single ci.yml with no continue-on-error. The team is asking 'what's the next layer to add?'
expected_behavior: Phase 1: maps all 8 layers and concludes most are GATED or ADVISORY-WITH-RATCHET in a healthy way
Applies the 'what would slip through' test for each absent or advisory layer and is honest when the answer is 'nothing concrete in this codebase'
Refuses to fabricate adoption candidates just to fill 5–8 slots — Phase 2 may legitimately produce 0–2 candidates here
If Phase 2 produces zero candidates, says so explicitly with the evidence: each layer's current state, why no upgrade has a concrete bug class to catch, and how the team would know when that changes
If Phase 2 produces 1–2 candidates (e.g. complexity composite — only layer absent), they each carry a concrete bug class, not a hypothetical
Does NOT propose tightening already-healthy gates (raising coverage from 85% to 90%) without a concrete bug class that the higher threshold catches
Suggests when to re-run /bruhs:drill — e.g. 'after adopting any module deepening from /bruhs:deepen' or 'on the next dependency-rules.json change'
Does NOT propose adding layers redundant with the existing healthy ones
failure_modes: Forces 5+ candidates despite the suite being healthy — pads the list with hypothetical bug classes
Recommends raising coverage from 85% → 95% without a concrete failure case
Treats a healthy ratchet-only mutation gate as 'advisory only' and proposes a hard gate when the team already chose the posture deliberately
Proposes a second acceptance-spec harness because the docker-compose one is 'unusual'
Misses the absence of a complexity composite (if it is in fact absent) because every other layer looks shiny
